# Spartan Gate

Spartan Gate is defensive Docker infrastructure for private Hermes-class AI
agents. It combines Hermes, ClawRoute, Caddy, Browserless/Camofox, Tinyproxy,
dnsmasq, SearXNG, and a bounded Reader service to reduce direct network,
credential, and browser exposure.

Read this first when orienting an LLM or coding agent to the repository.

## Start Here

- [README](README.md): product summary, quick start, tiers, and common commands.
- [Security](docs/security.md): threat model, controls, secrets, logging, and
  boundary ownership.
- [Layers](docs/layers.md): L0-L4 service model, addons, and Hermes modes.
- [Environment](docs/env.md): public environment variables and operator-facing
  contracts.
- [Operations](docs/operations.md): safe startup, helper aliases, profiles,
  logs, Browserless, Camofox, and recovery workflows.
- [Publishing](docs/publishing.md): public/private split, OSS readiness checks,
  and current readiness posture.

## Architecture

- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md): high-level service layout.
- [ClawRoute](docs/clawroute.md): OpenAI-compatible LLM router behavior.
- [Reader](docs/reader.md): GET-only web reader and untrusted-content contract.
- [Profiles](docs/profiles.md): Hermes and browser profile behavior.
- [Patches](docs/patches.md): Hermes patch ownership and runtime adaptation.
- [Development](docs/development.md): local development and validation commands.

## Main Code Areas

- `apps/hermes-agent/`: Hermes image adaptation, runtime wrappers, patches, and
  tests.
- `packages/clawroute/`: TypeScript LLM router, OpenAI-compatible API, provider
  routing, dashboards, and Vitest suite.
- `infra/compose/`: public Docker Compose topology and tier overlays.
- `infra/caddy/`: authenticated local/private edge routing.
- `infra/outbound-proxy/` and `infra/dns/`: whitelist-controlled HTTP and DNS
  egress.
- `infra/reader/`: bounded Python GET-only web reader.
- `infra/browserless/` and `infra/camofox/`: browser service integration.
- `config/clawroute/`: public runtime routing examples.
- `scripts/`: installer, doctor, aliases, and operator helpers.
- `private.example/`: publishable templates for ignored private configuration.

## Safety Rules For Agents

- Do not inspect, summarize, or publish ignored `private/`, `runtime/`, browser
  profile, OAuth, or token-bearing data unless the user explicitly asks for a
  safe local operation.
- Preserve fail-closed behavior for network, auth, policy, Reader, Caddy, and
  ClawRoute boundaries.
- Keep Hermes off external networks in gated tiers; use Tinyproxy/DNS/Reader for
  controlled egress.
- Update docs and `CHANGELOG.md` when operator-visible behavior changes.
- Validate the narrow changed surface first, then broaden according to blast
  radius.

## Validation Shortcuts

```sh
npm run clawroute:build
npm run clawroute:test
python3 -m unittest discover -s apps/hermes-agent -p "test_*.py"
node --test apps/hermes-agent/test_*.js
python3 -m unittest discover -s scripts -p "test_*.py"
python3 -m unittest discover -s infra/reader -p "test_*.py"
docker compose -f infra/compose/compose.yml config --quiet
scripts/doctor.sh
```

## Agent Readability Plan

The first agent-orientation artifact is this root `llms.txt`, because it is
small, easy to keep in sync, and does not require a separate generated wiki.
See [docs/agent-readability.md](docs/agent-readability.md) for the rollout plan
and criteria for adding a fuller LLM wiki later.
